summary: Launch workspace agent terminals with a configured PTY command and working directory instead of typing Set-Location and agent commands into an already-running shell.

review_items.summary: Direct agent terminal_open requests spawn a PTY process with the resolved workspace directory set as the process cwd and the agent command configured at process creation time.
Windows agent launches use PowerShell NoLogo/NoExit with a configured command argument so PowerShell does not visibly echo Set-Location or codex.cmd startup input.
Workspace startup no longer prewarms visible agent panes behind the startup gate; terminals open through the direct configured launch path once a ready agent is selected.
Authenticated paid gating, local terminal input, resize, close, and hosted API contracts are unchanged.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, or data access change is required.

patches.summary: Add configured PTY command builders for direct agent launch and cwd-aware shell creation.
Disable background idle shell pool warming now that visible agent panes use direct configured PTY process launch.
Use configured PTY process spawning for direct agent opens and keep shell input writing only for explicit deferred shell launches.
Stop mounting startup prewarm agent panes so startup terminals use the direct launch path after readiness completes.

compatibility.notes: The change is local to native desktop terminal startup and does not alter auth, billing, workspace persistence, SQL, or hosted API contracts.
